sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Vanguard (NASDAQ: SAFT) holds 760,299 Safety Insurance shares
feed.summary: Vanguard Capital Management reported beneficially owning 760,299 shares of Safety Insurance Group Inc. (SAFT) common stock, representing 5.19% of the class, as detailed in a Schedule 13G filing on April 30, 2026. This passive investment grants Vanguard sole voting power for 108,759 shares and sole dispositive power for all 760,299 shares. The filing assures that the shares were acquired in the ordinary course of business and not for controlling or influencing the issuer.

feed.title: UBS AG (AMUB) prices capped 160% participation MSCI EAFE notes
feed.summary: UBS AG has priced capped leveraged buffered MSCI EAFE index-linked medium-term notes (AMUB) with a 160% upside participation rate and a 15% buffer level. The notes, with a face value of $1,000, are expected to have a term between 25 and 28 months. Investors face the risk of leveraged losses if the underlying MSCI EAFE index falls below the 85% buffer level, and returns are capped at an expected maximum settlement amount between $1,257.12 and $1,302.40 per note, meaning they will not benefit from index gains above a certain cap.

feed.title: Winnebago Industries Appoints Emily Silver to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Winnebago Industries has appointed Emily Silver, Senior Vice President at DICK'S Sporting Goods, to its board of directors as an independent director, effective May 1st. Silver will also serve on the technology and human resources committees, bringing expertise in marketing, e-commerce, digital transformation, and consumer insights. Her extensive background in brand strategy and customer engagement from her time at PepsiCo and DICK'S Sporting Goods is expected to enhance Winnebago's long-term growth strategy and customer connection.

feed.title: Delta Passenger Who Claimed to Suffer Permanent Burns During Flight Settles with Airline
feed.summary: A Michigan woman, Kimberly Hickey, who sued Delta Air Lines after allegedly suffering second and third-degree burns from spilled coffee on a 2024 flight, has reached a settlement with the airline. Hickey claimed a flight attendant placed an "excessively hot" liquid on her "slanted tray table," leading to the spill and severe burns. Delta, however, contended that Hickey spilled the coffee herself and declined immediate assistance, including changing out of her wet clothing. The terms of the settlement, reached earlier this month, have not been disclosed.

feed.title: Whitestone REIT 2025 Executive Compensation, Governance, and Pay-for-Performance Practices Detailed in Amended 10-K/A Filing
feed.summary: Whitestone REIT has filed an amended 10-K/A for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, detailing executive compensation, corporate governance, and pay-for-performance practices. The filing also includes key financial highlights for 2025, such as increased revenues, net income, and Funds from Operations, and information regarding a merger agreement with Ares Real Estate funds. These updates aim to provide shareholders with transparent and crucial insights into the company's operations and future strategic direction.

feed.title: World Acceptance (WRLD) director sells $28.8K in open-market trade
feed.summary: Benjamin E. Robinson III, a director at World Acceptance Corp (WRLD), sold 180 shares of the company's common stock in an open-market transaction for approximately $28,800. The sale occurred at $160.00 per share, leaving him with 4,926 directly held shares. This transaction was made pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 plan.

feed.title: Assessing Regions Financial (RF) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
feed.summary: Regions Financial (RF) has seen recent share price momentum, with short-term gains and a 45.10% total shareholder return over the past year. The stock is currently trading at $28.55, which is about 7% below the average analyst price target and indicates a 52% intrinsic discount. The narrative suggests a fair value of $30.69, driven by digital banking investments and improved operational efficiency, but also acknowledges risks such as regional competition and potential shocks.

feed.title: Argus Adjusts Price Target on Thermo Fisher Scientific to $560 From $610
feed.summary: Argus has reduced its price target for Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) to $560, down from the previous $610. This adjustment comes amidst recent news, including Thermo Fisher's sale of its microbiology business to Astorg for approximately $1.1 billion. The article also lists other recent analyst price target changes for the company.

feed.title: Invesco to buy ETF provider Source
feed.summary: Invesco Ltd. has acquired Source, a European exchange-traded fund (ETF) provider, from Warburg Pincus and a group of banks. The transaction includes US$18 billion in Source-managed AUM and US$7 billion in externally managed AUM. This acquisition expands Invesco's ETF offerings, particularly in Europe, and is expected to close in the third quarter pending regulatory approvals.

feed.title: Southern Co. electricity sales soar on 42% data center growth
feed.summary: Southern Company reported a 2.3% year-over-year increase in retail electricity sales for Q1 this year, primarily driven by a 42% surge in data center power consumption. The company has 28 large load projects under contract, representing 11 GW, and its subsidiary, Georgia Power, increased Q1 capital spending to over $2 billion. Southern Company is also pursuing an additional 6 GW in large load customers and is involved in a $26.5 billion Department of Energy loan agreement for grid upgrades and new generation capacity.

feed.title: Undiscovered Gems In The US Market 3 Small Caps To Watch
feed.summary: This article identifies three small-cap "undiscovered gems" in the US market with strong growth potential, despite the overall market remaining flat over the last week. The featured companies are G. Willi-Food International (WILC), Seneca Foods (SENE.A), and Natural Grocers by Vitamin Cottage (NGVC), all highlighted for their robust financial health, impressive earnings growth, and attractive valuations. The piece also provides a broader list of other small-cap stocks with strong fundamentals.

feed.title: PayPal Q1 Earnings Preview: P/E Multiple Lags Behind The Business Slowdown (PYPL)
feed.summary: The author reiterates a "sell" rating for PayPal, arguing that its low P/E multiple is deceptive due to declining EPS and shrinking margins, suggesting the stock isn't as undervalued as it appears. Structural industry challenges and increased competition are weakening PayPal's market position, with anticipated drops in transaction volume per account and take rates. The article concludes that unless the new CEO can successfully turn the company around, further earnings declines are expected.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries (NYSE: MHK) lifts Q1 2026 sales, earnings and cash flow
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries reported a significant increase in Q1 2026 sales, earnings, and cash flow. Net sales rose to $2,728.7 million, with net earnings attributable to Mohawk increasing to $117.1 million and diluted EPS reaching $1.90. The improved performance was attributed to higher shipping days, favorable foreign exchange, and productivity gains, despite ongoing challenges from lower volume and higher input costs in the housing market.

feed.title: Beyond medication: A whole person approach to lasting weight loss
feed.summary: This article highlights that sustained weight loss often requires more than just medication, promoting a comprehensive approach that integrates medical therapy with nutritional, behavioral, and lifestyle support. The CVS Weight Management program exemplifies this by combining GLP-1 medications with personalized coaching and accountability, as demonstrated by Mistie Mace's successful journey. This holistic method aims to foster long-term healthy habits and optimize weight management outcomes.

feed.title: All Rhode Island CVS Pharmacy® locations now accept WIC for infant formula
feed.summary: CVS Pharmacy announced that all 56 of its locations in Rhode Island now accept the Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) for infant formula purchases. This makes Rhode Island the first state where every CVS Pharmacy store is approved for the program, aimed at removing barriers for families needing infant nutrition support. WIC benefits currently apply to infant formula only, and CVS Pharmacy is working to expand WIC authorization in other states.

feed.title: Titan International to Participate in Oppenheimer Conference
feed.summary: Titan International's CEO Paul Reitz will participate in a fireside chat at the Oppenheimer Industrial Growth Virtual Conference on May 5, 2026, at 2:15 PM ET, offering investor interaction and enhancing the company's visibility. The event will be streamed live and available for replay, ensuring broad access to information about the leading global manufacturer of off-highway wheels and tires. This participation aims to strengthen investor relationships and support future financing and expansion.

feed.title: Edison International (NYSE:EIX) Stock Price Expected to Rise, JPMorgan Chase & Co. Analyst Says
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has increased its price target for Edison International (NYSE:EIX) to $75 from $74, maintaining a "neutral" rating and suggesting a roughly 7.9% upside. While the consensus analyst rating remains a "Hold" with an average target of $72.82, the company recently beat EPS expectations, reporting $1.42 against an anticipated $1.32, and set a positive FY2026 EPS guidance. Several hedge funds have also significantly increased their holdings in Edison International, indicating growing institutional interest.

feed.title: Ecolab Life Sciences Opens Bioprocessing Applications Lab In Korea To Support Downstream Process Development Across Asia
feed.summary: Ecolab Life Sciences has opened a new Bioprocessing Applications Lab (BPAL) in Dongtan, Korea, to provide local downstream process development support to biopharmaceutical manufacturers across Asia. This facility, Ecolab's first bioprocessing site in Asia, will help optimize chromatography steps, improve yield, and accelerate regulatory pathways. It aims to support the region's rapidly growing biopharmaceutical industry, particularly in biosimilars and monoclonal antibodies, by offering local technical assistance and expertise.

feed.title: Builders FirstSource (NYSE:BLDR) Given New $107.00 Price Target at Royal Bank Of Canada
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada lowered its price target for Builders FirstSource (NYSE:BLDR) to $107 from $110, while maintaining an "outperform" rating, suggesting a 38.4% upside. This adjustment follows mixed Q1 results where revenue beat estimates but EPS missed, alongside a weaker 2026 outlook. Despite analyst target cuts, the company's board authorized a $500 million share buyback and an insider purchased 50,000 shares, signaling management confidence.

feed.title: Philadelphia's cycling race returns after 10 years, backed by AmeriGas
feed.summary: AmeriGas Propane, a UGI Corporation subsidiary, is sponsoring the return of the Philadelphia Cycling Classic on August 30, 2026, after a decade-long hiatus. Ahead of the race, AmeriGas hosted a "Philly 250 Forum" bringing together city leaders to discuss major 2026 events including the FIFA World Cup and the nation's 250th anniversary. This sponsorship is part of AmeriGas's ongoing momentum and commitment to the city, providing equal prize purses for men's and women's professional fields.

feed.title: Iron Mountain (NYSE:IRM) Price Target Raised to $138.00 at JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has raised its price target for Iron Mountain (NYSE:IRM) to $138.00 from $121.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating and suggesting an 8.64% upside. This follows a strong Q1 earnings report where Iron Mountain exceeded analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, and subsequently raised its full-year 2026 EPS guidance. However, the report also notes significant insider selling and a stretched valuation, presenting potential risks despite the bullish outlook.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Issues Positive Forecast for Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE:APD) Stock Price
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. raised its price target for Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE:APD) stock from $310 to $330, maintaining an "overweight" rating. This positive outlook follows Air Products' strong Q2 earnings, which surpassed estimates, and an upward revision of its FY2026 guidance. While several other analysts have also increased their price targets, concerns remain regarding insider selling and the company's debt-to-equity ratio.

feed.title: VolitionRx Limited 2025 Annual Report: Executive Compensation, Director Ownership, and Corporate Governance Insights
feed.summary: VolitionRx Ltd. (NYSE American: VNRX) has filed an Amended Annual Report for 2025, detailing a one-for-twenty reverse stock split, updated executive and director compensation, board structure, and insider ownership. The reverse split, effective April 28, 2026, significantly reduced outstanding shares, impacting per-share value and liquidity. Investors are advised to monitor these changes and subsequent SEC filings for further developments.

feed.title: BondBloxx B rated USD high yield corporate bond ETF declares monthly distribution of $0.2303
feed.summary: BondBloxx B Rated USD High Yield Corporate Bond ETF (NYSEARCA:XHYB) has declared a monthly distribution of $0.2303 per share. This distribution is payable on May 8, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 3, 2026, with an ex-dividend date of May 2, 2026. The ex-dividend date is one business day before the record date.

feed.title: Boeing Buys 20,000-Ton Portfolio of Biochar, ERW Carbon Removals
feed.summary: Boeing has purchased a 20,000-ton portfolio of durable carbon dioxide removal (CDR) credits from Supercritical, sourced from biochar and enhanced rock weathering (ERW) projects in developing countries. This move supports Boeing's strategy to address hard-to-abate Scope 3 emissions related to business travel and reinforces its "avoid first, remove second" carbon management approach. The diversified portfolio includes six suppliers in Brazil, Bolivia, Namibia, and India, selected through a rigorous 118-point scientific framework.

feed.title: Royal Bank Of Canada Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) Stock Price
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada has cut its price target for Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) from $245 to $200 but maintained an "outperform" rating, suggesting a potential 28% upside. Despite this, analyst sentiment is mixed, with other firms also trimming targets, leading to a "Moderate Buy" consensus. Broadridge recently beat Q3 earnings estimates and issued strong FY-2026 guidance, while key insider buying also indicates management confidence.

feed.title: SEC Form 424B3 filed by JP Morgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: This article reports that JP Morgan Chase & Co. (JPM) filed an SEC Form 424B3, as tracked by Quantisnow. Quantisnow is a platform that delivers real-time market data, including SEC filings, to retail investors. The article also provides recent analyst ratings for JPM and lists other related SEC filings.

feed.title: Intuit MailChimp Sued Over Patents for Text Message Transactions
feed.summary: Intuit MailChimp is being sued by IQX Corp. over alleged infringement of patents related to secure text message transactions. The lawsuit, filed in the US District Court for the Eastern District of Texas, claims that MailChimp's technology infringes on IQX's patents for secure access to in-progress transactions via third-party messaging and a "two-document solution" for completing transactions without exposing data to insecure networks.

feed.title: Nasdaq Composite Posts Best Month In 6 Years As AI Boom Powers Tech Surge — Alphabet, Intel Stocks Power The Super Rally
feed.summary: The Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 indices experienced their best months in years during April 2026, driven by an AI boom and strong performance from technology giants like Alphabet and Intel. The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund also saw significant gains, reflecting the robust blended earnings growth in the information technology sector. Analysts note that investors view the industry as a safe way to participate in the AI trade.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts Price Target on Brown & Brown to $65 From $70
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for Brown & Brown (BRO) shares to $65 from $70. This adjustment follows a series of similar revisions from other financial institutions, including Mizuho, Wells Fargo, BMO Capital, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods, Jefferies, Goldman Sachs, and Citigroup, mostly occurring around late April and early May 2026. The stock is currently trading at $58.96, reflecting a recent decline.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Wabash National misses Q1 2026 EPS, stock declines
feed.summary: Wabash National (WNC) reported a significant miss on Q1 2026 EPS and revenue, leading to a stock decline. The company posted an EPS of -$1.17 against a forecast of -$1.01 and revenue of $303 million versus $319 million. Despite the challenging quarter, Wabash National anticipates sequential improvements throughout 2026, focusing on strategic initiatives and cost management.

feed.title: US FDA approves Pfizer, Arvinas' breast cancer drug
feed.summary: The U.S. FDA has approved Veppanu, a breast cancer drug developed by Pfizer and Arvinas, for adults with an advanced form of estrogen receptor-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer with an ESR1 mutation. This approval is for patients whose cancer has progressed after at least one prior hormone treatment. Following the announcement, Arvinas' shares rose 3.7%, while Pfizer's stock saw a slight dip of about 1%.

feed.title: Fidelity lists 12,888 Nucor shares for resale (NUE)
feed.summary: Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC has filed a Section 144 notice for Nucor (NUE), indicating a proposed sale of 12,888 common shares. This filing is a notice of intent to sell rather than an executed transaction. The shares include 5,149 from restricted stock vesting on March 10, 2026, and 7,739 from an option granted on June 1, 2021, with an exercise date of May 1, 2026.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Saia (NASDAQ:SAIA) Stock
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has increased its price target for Saia (NASDAQ:SAIA) stock from $402.00 to $465.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating and suggesting a 4.3% upside. This adjustment follows Saia's slight beat on Q1 estimates with an EPS of $1.86 and revenue of $806.2 million, alongside management's optimistic outlook for sales and margin rebound. Despite mixed analyst views, with an average "Hold" rating and a target of $433.39, several other firms have also raised their price targets for the transportation company.

feed.title: Humana (NYSE:HUM) Given New $214.00 Price Target at JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has raised its price target for Humana (NYSE:HUM) to $214.00 from $180.00 while maintaining a "neutral" rating, suggesting a potential 9.74% downside from its previous close. Other analysts have also adjusted their targets, leading to a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $245.96. The company recently beat Q1 earnings estimates, but management's warning of margin pressures and declining Medicare Advantage Star ratings has introduced uncertainty regarding its near-term outlook and profit recovery.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts Illinois Tool Works Price Target to $287 From $284
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Illinois Tool Works (ITW) to $287 from $284. This adjustment comes shortly after Illinois Tool Works also saw a price target increase from Goldman Sachs and reported positive Q1 2026 earnings, leading to an increased 2026 EPS guidance.

feed.title: BMO Capital Adjusts Price Target on Xcel Energy to $95 From $94
feed.summary: BMO Capital has increased its price target for Xcel Energy (XEL) to $95 from $94, as reported by MT Newswires. The article highlights recent activities surrounding Xcel Energy, including its Q1 2026 earnings where it narrowly beat profit estimates on stronger electricity sales and reaffirmed its 2026 guidance. Other analyst target adjustments from Morgan Stanley and KeyBanc are also noted.

feed.title: [ARS] F&G Annuities & Life, Inc. SEC Filing
feed.summary: This article announces an ARS SEC filing by F&G Annuities & Life, Inc. (FG) on April 30, 2026. The filing is available as a PDF document on SEC EDGAR and is categorized with a low impact and neutral sentiment. The article also provides recent news and SEC filings for FG, along with key stock data such as market cap and industry.

feed.title: Trustmark (TRMK) director awarded 1,396 shares of common stock
feed.summary: Augustus L. Collins, a director at Trustmark Corp (TRMK), was granted 1,396 shares of common stock on April 28, 2026, as a compensation-related award with no cash cost. Following this transaction, Collins directly holds a total of 15,430 shares, which includes shares from an employee stock purchase plan and a dividend reinvestment plan. This Form 4 filing indicates an acquisition of shares rather than an open-market purchase.

feed.title: Transcript: Wabash National Q1 2026 Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: Wabash National reported Q1 2026 revenue of $303 million, falling below guidance, with an adjusted non-GAAP EBITDA of negative $38 million due to reduced production volumes. Despite market softness, the company anticipates recovery, forecasting Q2 2026 revenue between $380 million and $400 million and improved EPS. Strategic initiatives, including digital enablement and parts/services expansion, are underway to position Wabash National for market recovery and long-term growth, with optimism for 2027 as freight indicators improve.

feed.title: Winnebago Industries Adds Emily Silver to Board
feed.summary: Winnebago Industries (WGO) has appointed Emily Silver, a senior executive at DICK'S Sporting Goods and former PepsiCo marketing leader, to its board of directors as an independent Class I director. Silver's expertise in digital marketing, e-commerce, and brand strategy is expected to enhance Winnebago's focus on digital engagement and growth in the outdoor recreation market. The company目前 has a Buy rating from analysts with a $43.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates WGO as Neutral due to profitability concerns despite a supportive balance sheet and positive free cash flow.

feed.title: Veritone Inc Stock (US92343X1063): Analyst Upgrade and Price Target Revision
feed.summary: Veritone Inc (VERI) stock has risen following an analyst upgrade and revised price target, reflecting renewed investor interest in its AI-powered media and content platform. The upgrade is attributed to the company's improving financial performance, expanding recurring revenue, and strategic partnerships, particularly with its aiWARE platform used across sports, media, and government sectors. Despite recent volatility attributed to market sentiment and quarterly results, the company's focus on AI solutions for unstructured data and strong recurring revenue growth position it for long-term growth in a rapidly expanding industry.

feed.title: Short Interest in Waterdrop Inc. Unsponsored ADR (NYSE:WDH) Drops By 14.2%
feed.summary: Waterdrop Inc. (NYSE:WDH) experienced a 14.2% decrease in short interest in April, bringing the total to 95,586 shares with a low days-to-cover ratio of 0.6 days. Despite a recent downgrade to "Hold" by Wall Street Zen and a consensus price target of $2.00, the company surpassed quarterly earnings estimates with an EPS of $0.06 and revenue of $201.7 million, and also announced a $0.03 dividend.

feed.title: Citi Maintains Teradata Corp(TDC.US) With Buy Rating, Cuts Target Price to $38
feed.summary: Citi analysts have reiterated a Buy rating on Teradata Corp (TDC.US) but have revised their target price downwards to $38. This adjustment suggests a continued positive outlook on the company despite the reduced price target, indicating potential challenges or updated financial models.

feed.title: Viking Therapeutics Faces Timeline Risk—But Upside Could Be Huge
feed.summary: This article analyzes Sherwin-Williams' Q1 2026 earnings, which beat expectations despite a soft housing market and rising input costs. The company, a Dividend Aristocrat, faces valuation concerns with its P/E ratio higher than the market and its sector, but showcases a strong balance sheet. Its long-term compounding ability is highlighted, with potential for accelerated growth if mortgage rates decline.

feed.title: Why Generac Stock Powered Higher This Week
feed.summary: Generac (GNRC) shares surged this week by approximately 17% after the company reported strong quarterly earnings and raised its full-year sales forecast. The growth was primarily driven by a 28% sales increase in its commercial and industrial (C&I) segment, which is benefiting from the expanding data center market's need for off-grid and backup power solutions. While the housing market remains sluggish, Generac anticipates an increase in residential sales, further contributing to its optimistic outlook.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on AT&T: “It Gives You the Income, But the Growth Side Is Not There”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on AT&T (NYSE: T), advising a caller who owns the stock that while it provides income, it lacks growth. He suggested swapping AT&T for a master limited partnership like Enterprise Products or ONEOK, which offer both growth and income. Cramer also previously cautioned against investing in the crowded telecommunications sector including AT&T due to "too many crosscurrents."

feed.title: $6B Morgan Stanley Team Jumps to Wells Fargo Advisors in NYC
feed.summary: A Morgan Stanley team managing $5.94 billion in assets and generating $18.6 million in annual revenue, led by C. James Taylor, has moved to Wells Fargo Advisors in New York City. The 19-person "Taylor Group" specializes in clients from Nvidia, whose stock surge contributed to their rapid asset growth. Wells Fargo is reportedly offering aggressive incentives to attract high-growth teams and recently attributed its recruiting success to a multi-channel approach.

feed.title: Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) Hits New 52-Week High - Still a Buy?
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) recently reached a new 52-week high of $353.52, driven by a strong earnings beat, with EPS of $2.17 against estimates of $2.00 and a 23.3% YoY revenue increase. Despite insider selling activity by CFO Neil Dougherty and CEO Satish Dhanasekaran, institutional investors have increased their stakes, owning 84.58% of the company. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $314.33, suggesting the stock might still be considered a buy despite exceeding price targets.

feed.title: Hershey tops estimates as GLP‑1 users drive demand for mints, healthier snacks
feed.summary: Hershey exceeded first-quarter earnings estimates, driven by increased demand for its healthier snack options and mints, a trend partly fueled by GLP-1 weight-loss drug users. The company reported an 8% rise in retail sales for its Ice Breakers brand, as GLP-1 users seek mints for "Ozempic breath," and highlighted its focus on "functional snacking" as a growth strategy. Despite a decline in North America's confectionery segment, Hershey reiterated its annual sales and profit growth targets.
